docs.title: API Reference
docs.text: This page provides detailed documentation for the API endpoints available in this project.

docs.title: Authentication
docs.text:
All API requests require authentication using an API key.

curl -H "Authorization: Bearer YOUR_API_KEY" https://api.example.com/v1/resource

docs.title: GET /api/v1/resources
docs.text:
Returns a list of all resources.

Parameters:

Name Type Description limit integer Maximum number of resources to return offset integer Number of resources to skip

Example Response:

{
  "data": [
    {
      "id": "1",
      "name": "Example Resource",
      "created_at": "2024-01-01T12:00:00Z"
    }
  ],
  "meta": {
    "total": 100,
    "limit": 10,
    "offset": 0
  }
}

docs.title: POST /api/v1/resources
docs.text:
Creates a new resource.

Request Body:

{
  "name": "New Resource",
  "description": "A description of the resource"
}

Example Response:

{
  "id": "2",
  "name": "New Resource",
  "description": "A description of the resource",
  "created_at": "2024-05-20T14:30:00Z"
}

docs.title: Contributing Guide
docs.text: We welcome contributions to our project! This document provides guidelines for contributing to our codebase.

docs.title: Setting Up Development Environment
docs.text:
1. Fork the repository
  2. Clone your fork: 
    git clone https://github.com/your-username/project-name.git
cd project-name
  3. Install dependencies: 
    pip install -r requirements.txt
  4. Install development dependencies: 
    pip install -r requirements-dev.txt

docs.title: Code Style
docs.text:
We follow PEP 8 style guidelines for Python code. Please ensure your code adheres to these standards.

# Run the linter
flake8 .

# Run the formatter
black .

docs.title: Pull Request Process
docs.text:
1. Create a new branch for your feature or bugfix
  2. Make your changes and commit them with clear, descriptive messages
  3. Push your branch to your fork
  4. Submit a pull request to our main repository
  5. Address any code review feedback

docs.title: Running Tests
docs.text:
Before submitting a pull request, make sure all tests pass:

pytest

docs.title: Documentation
docs.text: If your changes require documentation updates, please include them in your pull request.

docs.title: Code of Conduct
docs.text: Please note that this project is released with a Contributor Code of Conduct. By participating in this project, you agree to abide by its terms.

docs.title: Welcome
docs.text: Welcome to my website! This is a place where I share my thoughts, ideas, and experiences. I hope you find something interesting here.

docs.title: Understand
docs.text: First understand the problem/process what ever it is. Even understand something how it works.

docs.title: Implement

docs.title: Share
docs.text: Share your knowledge/experience with others. It can be in the form of writing, speaking, teaching, etc. It is a way to learn and understand better. Of course, failure experiments as well

docs.title: Collaborate
docs.text: Collaborate with others to solve the problem. It is a way to learn and understand better.

About Me

A result-driven and proven Technical Engineering Lead with 11+ years of experience in FinTech (FX and Collateral Management). Specialized in building configurable and reusable products. Passionate about design, documentation, automation, and mentorship.

Roles and Responsibilities

My entire journey has been with start-ups, allowing me to take on multiple roles and responsibilities. Each role has contributed to my expertise in software development, leadership, product strategy, and automation.

  • Data Engineer: Built and deployed robust data pipelines using AWS services such as Glue, Lambda, DataBrew, and QuickSight.
  • Mentor: Trained and guided freshers, fostering a knowledge-sharing culture and accelerating their technical and professional growth.
  • Development Manager: Aligned team objectives with project goals, driving productivity, collaboration, and long-term career success.
  • Product Manager: Engaged with clients to gather requirements, define product vision and roadmaps, and ensure customer satisfaction.
  • Automation Tester: Developed an AI-driven testing agent to automate test generation and execution, enhancing software reliability.

Software Engineer

docs.title: What is AI Agents?
docs.text:
In simple term, Computer Agent (AI Agent) is something can act like kind of program that can perform some autonomus actions like searching, analysing and taking some decision and provide you the end results.

Don't think too complex, Lets think the below

You wanted to book a Cab, There are multiple platform to book a cab (Ola, Uber, Rapido). How to find the low cost and book the cab.

Currently, we need to open all the apps and provide the source and destinaiton and check the price. Step 1: Open Ola provide the source and destination and check the price. (Remind the price) Step 2: Open Uber provide the source and destination and check the price. (Remind the price) Step 3: Open Rapido provide the source and destination and check the price. (Remind the price)

Two hassale for the above method 1. Time consuming 2. Keep the price in mind 3. There are few repetative operation

Now, Agent can perform the above operation behalf of you. Just instruct the Agent to do the Cab price analysis and book the van. It can analyse the price and book the cab without human intervention.

You may think that can be done even in the piece of code, Obsolutly correc but the problem here. Let assume you wrote a piece of code that can analyse and book the cab. After few weeks you wanted to book a cab for later. Now you have to modify your code to introduce the new feature. In the AI Agent can to do for you without making any code changes.

Hope you got some idea about what is Agent.

docs.title: Topics Covered
docs.text:
• What is Complexity Analysis?
  • Why is Complexity Analysis Important?
  • Key Concepts: Space and Time Complexity
  • Big-O Notation and Examples
  • Big O Complexity Chart
  • Tips and Techniques to Determine and Differentiate Complexities

docs.title: What is Complexity Analysis?
docs.text: Complexity analysis involves evaluating the efficiency of algorithms in terms of time and space. It helps in understanding how the algorithm's performance scales with input size.

docs.title: Why is Complexity Analysis Important?
docs.text:
• Performance Evaluation: Determines how algorithms perform with large datasets.
  • Optimization: Helps in choosing the most efficient algorithm for a given problem.
  • Scalability: Ensures that solutions remain effective as data size grows.
  • Resource Management: Aids in efficient utilization of computational resources.

docs.title: Key Concepts: Space and Time Complexity
docs.text:
• Time Complexity: Measures the amount of time an algorithm takes to complete as a function of the input size.
  • Space Complexity: Measures the amount of memory space an algorithm uses during its execution.

docs.title: Big-O Notation and Examples
docs.text:
Big-O notation describes the upper bound of an algorithm's running time. Common complexities include:

  • O(1): Constant time
  • O(log n): Logarithmic time
  • O(n): Linear time
  • O(n log n): Linearithmic time
  • O(n²): Quadratic time
  • O(2ⁿ): Exponential time

Examples:

  • O(1): Accessing an element in an array by index.
  • O(n): Traversing a list.
  • O(n²): Nested loops over the same dataset.

docs.title: Big O Complexity Chart of Common Data Structure Operations
docs.text:
Common Data Structure Operations

Data Structure Average Access Average Search Average Insertion Average Deletion Worst Access Worst Search Worst Insertion Worst Deletion Space Complexity Array O(1) O(n) O(n) O(n) O(1) O(n) O(n) O(n) O(n) Stack O(n) O(n) O(1) O(1) O(n) O(n) O(1) O(1) O(n) Queue O(n) O(n) O(1) O(1) O(n) O(n) O(1) O(1) O(n) Singly Linked List O(n) O(n) O(1) O(1) O(n) O(n) O(1) O(1) O(n) Doubly Linked List O(n) O(n) O(1) O(1) O(n) O(n) O(1) O(1) O(n) Skip List O(log n) O(log n) O(log n) O(log n) O(n) O(n) O(n) O(n) O(n log n) Hash Table N/A O(1) O(1) O(1) N/A O(n) O(n) O(n) O(n) Binary Search Tree O(log n) O(log n) O(log n) O(log n) O(n) O(n) O(n) O(n) O(n) Cartesian Tree N/A O(log n) O(log n) O(log n) N/A O(n) O(n) O(n) O(n) B-Tree O(log n) O(log n) O(log n) O(log n) O(log n) O(log n) O(log n) O(log n) O(n) Red-Black Tree O(log n) O(log n) O(log n) O(log n) O(log n) O(log n) O(log n) O(log n) O(n) Splay Tree N/A O(log n) O(log n) O(log n) N/A O(log n) O(log n) O(log n) O(n) AVL Tree O(log n) O(log n) O(log n) O(log n) O(log n) O(log n) O(log n) O(log n) O(n) KD Tree O(log n) O(log n) O(log n) O(log n) O(n) O(n) O(n) O(n) O(n)

docs.title: Reference
docs.text: For a comprehensive overview, visit the original article: Day 4 of 30 Days of Data Structures and Algorithms and System Design Simplified — Complexity Analysis

docs.title: Tips and Techniques to Determine and Differentiate Complexities
docs.text:
• Identify Loops: Single loops often indicate linear complexity, nested loops may suggest quadratic complexity.
  • Recursive Calls: Analyze the recurrence relation to determine complexity.
  • Divide and Conquer: Algorithms that divide the problem into subproblems often have logarithmic or linearithmic complexity.
  • Space Usage: Consider additional data structures used and their sizes relative to input.

docs.title: Understanding Version Control and Git
docs.text: Version control is one of the foundational tools every software developer should master. In this two-part blog, we'll explore what version control is and how Git helps implement it effectively.

docs.title: What is the Problem?
docs.text:
When working on software projects, especially with a team, some common problems arise:

  • Overwriting code: Two people edit the same file, and one person’s changes are lost.
  • Tracking changes: It’s hard to know what changed, when, and why.
  • Reverting changes: If something breaks, it’s not easy to go back to a previous working version.
  • Managing multiple versions: Handling different features or fixes at the same time becomes messy.

docs.title: How Version Control Solves the Problem?
docs.text:
Version control systems (VCS) are tools that:

  • Track changes over time — every time you save a new version, it's recorded.
  • Allow collaboration — multiple people can work on the same project without conflict.
  • Enable rollback — go back to any previous version when needed.
  • Support branching — work on different features or bug fixes in parallel without interfering with the main project.

Version control provides a history of your project. Think of it as a "time machine for code."

docs.title: Part 2: Git
docs.text: Git is the most widely used version control system today. It's fast, flexible, and works both online and offline. Let’s dive into how Git helps manage version control.

docs.title: How Git Implements Version Control
docs.text:
Git stores your project as a series of snapshots, not just differences. Every time you commit, Git takes a snapshot of your files and stores a reference to it.

Git also works distributed — everyone has a full copy of the project history. That means you can work even when you’re not connected to the internet.

docs.title: Common Git Terminologies
docs.text: Here are some basic Git terms you'll encounter often:

docs.title: Repository
docs.text:
A repository (repo) is where your project lives. It includes all your files and the entire history of changes.

# Create a new repository
git init

docs.title: Clone
docs.text:
Cloning is copying a remote repository to your local machine.

git clone https://github.com/username/project.git

docs.title: Add
docs.text:
When you change files, you need to tell Git to track them by staging them.

git add file.txt      # Add a specific file
git add .             # Add all files in the directory

docs.title: Commit
docs.text:
Committing means saving your staged changes to the local repo with a message.

git commit -m "Add login feature"

docs.title: Push
docs.text:
Push sends your local commits to the remote repository (like GitHub).

git push origin main

docs.title: Pull
docs.text:
Pull brings the latest changes from the remote repository to your local repo.

git pull origin main

docs.title: Merge
docs.text:
Merge combines changes from different branches.

git checkout main       # Switch to the main branch
git merge feature-xyz   # Merge feature branch into main

docs.title: Example Workflow
docs.text:
git clone https://github.com/username/project.git
cd project

# Make changes to files
git add .
git commit -m "Fix UI bug"

# Get latest changes from others
git pull origin main

# Push your changes
git push origin main

docs.title: Conclusion
docs.text:
Version control is not just for big teams — it's essential for any coding project. Git makes it powerful, efficient, and accessible. Mastering these basics will make you a better collaborator and coder.

In the next blog, we’ll cover more advanced Git workflows like branching strategies, resolving merge conflicts, and working with pull requests.

docs.title: Why Computer Programming is Important? What is the Need?
docs.text:
I took an example if Banking to explain the need for computer programming, So that every one can easily understand the need for computer programming.

Let me explain the evolution of banking, which is a great example of how technology has transformed industries.

Bank Visits: Earlier, checking balance or transferring money required visiting a bank and waiting in long queues.

ATMs: Automated Teller Machines (ATMs) allowed customers to withdraw cash, check balances, and deposit money without entering a bank.

Online Banking: Internet banking enabled users to manage accounts, transfer funds, and pay bills from anywhere using secure websites.

NEFT & RTGS: Electronic fund transfers like NEFT and RTGS allowed faster and more secure money transfers between banks.

IMPS: Immediate Payment Service (IMPS) introduced real-time 24/7 money transfers, making banking instant and seamless.

UPI: Unified Payments Interface (UPI) enabled instant transactions using mobile numbers and QR codes, simplifying digital payments.

Chat based banking: Chat-based banking services like Google Pay and PhonePe's chatbots now allow users to check balances, transfer funds, and perform banking tasks via messaging apps.

Now you can understand the need for computer programming in our daily life. It's just a simple example, but it shows how programming has transformed various aspects of our lives.

docs.title: What is Computer Programming?
docs.text:
Let say If we want to coommunicate with someone, we need to know their language. Similarly, to communicate with a computer, we need to use a programming language.

If I wanted to communicate with you, I would need to know English. Similarly, to communicate with a computer, we need to use a programming language like Python, Java, or C++.

Likewise, I have a hardware (computer), How should I tell a computer to do something? That's where programming comes in. A small and basic illustration of programming is shown below:

Programming language allows developers to create software applications, automate processes, analyze data, and build intelligent systems.

docs.title: Configuration
docs.text: After installation, you'll need to configure the project for your specific needs.

docs.title: Basic Configuration
docs.text:
Create a configuration file named config.yaml in your project root directory:

# Basic configuration
app_name: MyApp
debug: false
log_level: info

# Database settings
database:
  host: localhost
  port: 5432
  name: mydb
  user: user
  password: password

docs.title: Environment Variables
docs.text:
You can also use environment variables to override configuration settings:

export APP_DEBUG=true
export APP_LOG_LEVEL=debug

docs.title: Advanced Options
docs.text: For advanced configuration options, please refer to the Advanced Usage section.

docs.title: Installation
docs.text: This guide will walk you through the process of installing the project.

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text:
• Python 3.7 or higher
  • pip (Python package manager)

docs.title: Step 1: Install via pip
docs.text: pip install your-package-name

docs.title: Step 2: Verify Installation
docs.text:
To verify that the installation was successful, run:

your-package-name --version

docs.title: Next Steps
docs.text: Once you have successfully installed the package, you can proceed to the Configuration section to set up your environment.

docs.title: Advanced Usage
docs.text: This section covers advanced features and usage scenarios for experienced users.

docs.title: Custom Extensions
docs.text:
You can extend the functionality of the system by creating custom extensions:

from myapp.extensions import Extension

class MyCustomExtension(Extension):
    def initialize(self):
        # Extension initialization logic
        pass

    def process(self, data):
        # Custom processing logic
        return modified_data

docs.title: Performance Optimization
docs.text:
For high-traffic applications, consider these optimization techniques:

  1. Caching: Implement a caching strategy for frequently accessed data
  2. Connection Pooling: Use connection pools for database operations
  3. Asynchronous Processing: Offload heavy tasks to background workers

docs.title: Scaling Strategies
docs.text:
When scaling your application, consider:

  • Horizontal scaling for web servers
  • Vertical scaling for database servers
  • Microservice architecture for complex applications

docs.title: User Guide Overview
docs.text: This user guide provides comprehensive information about how to use the project effectively.

docs.title: Basic Concepts
docs.text:
Before diving into the details, it's important to understand the basic concepts of our system:

  • Components: The building blocks of our application
  • Workflows: How different components interact with each other
  • Configurations: How to customize the behavior of the system

docs.title: Use Cases
docs.text:
Here are some common use cases:

  1. Setting up a development environment
  2. Creating your first project
  3. Deploying to production

docs.title: Best Practices
docs.text:
Following these best practices will help you get the most out of our system:

  • Keep your configurations simple
  • Use version control for your projects
  • Follow the recommended project structure
